content.thinking:
The root cause is clear:
- Total records: 100,000
- Hot user_id: 999
- Hot user records: 50,000 (50% of all records)
- Skew ratio: 500x (extremely high, well beyond 10x threshold)

This causes OOM when Stage 3 aggregates by user_id because all 50,000 records for user_id 999 end up on a single executor which runs out of the 256MB memory.

Now let me wait for the job to fail and then check the error message. But let me also look at the ETL job YAML to understand the current configuration.

content.text: Root cause confirmed: user_id 999 holds 50% of all records (skew ratio: 500x). Let me check the current job configuration, then wait for it to fail.

apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  annotations:
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"batch/v1","kind":"Job","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"etl-job","namespace":"spark-etl"},"spec":{"backoffLimit":0,"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"etl-job"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["/bin/bash","-c","set -e\nexport SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)\necho \"Stage 1: load raw data\"\necho \"Stage 2: clean and transform\"\necho \"Stage 3: aggregate by user_id (skewed on user_id 999)\"\n# --executor-memory 256m is too small for the skewed Stage 3 aggregation\n# and triggers OutOfMemoryError. The agent must raise it (e.g. 1g),\n# scale workers, or enable AQE skew handling before re-running.\n/opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \\\n  --master local[2] \\\n  --conf spark.executor.memory=1g \\\n  --conf spark.sql.adaptive.enabled=true \\\n  --conf spark.sql.adaptive.skewJoin.enabled=true \\\n  --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \\\n  /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \\\n  500\necho \"spark_etl_pipeline_verified\"\n"],"image":"apache/spark:3.5.3","name":"spark-etl","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"2Gi"},"requests":{"cpu":"200m","memory":"512Mi"}}}],"restartPolicy":"Never","serviceAccountName":"spark"}}}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-06-24T05:56:35Z"
  generation: 1
  labels:
    app: etl-job
    batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: 09a71c6b-5dbb-4f1f-b721-e6f790e6afc5
    batch.kubernetes.io/job-name: etl-job
    controller-uid: 09a71c6b-5dbb-4f1f-b721-e6f790e6afc5
    job-name: etl-job
  name: etl-job
  namespace: spark-etl
  resourceVersion: "952503"
  uid: 09a71c6b-5dbb-4f1f-b721-e6f790e6afc5
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  completionMode: NonIndexed
  completions: 1
  manualSelector: false
  parallelism: 1
  podReplacementPolicy: TerminatingOrFailed
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: 09a71c6b-5dbb-4f1f-b721-e6f790e6afc5
  suspend: false
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: etl-job
        batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: 09a71c6b-5dbb-4f1f-b721-e6f790e6afc5
        batch.kubernetes.io/job-name: etl-job
        controller-uid: 09a71c6b-5dbb-4f1f-b721-e6f790e6afc5
        job-name: etl-job
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -e
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          echo "Stage 1: load raw data"
          echo "Stage 2: clean and transform"
          echo "Stage 3: aggregate by user_id (skewed on user_id 999)"
          # --executor-memory 256m is too small for the skewed Stage 3 aggregation
          # and triggers OutOfMemoryError. The agent must raise it (e.g. 1g),
          # scale workers, or enable AQE skew handling before re-running.
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --conf spark.executor.memory=1g \
            --conf spark.sql.adaptive.enabled=true \
            --conf spark.sql.adaptive.skewJoin.enabled=true \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            500
          echo "spark_etl_pipeline_verified"
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: spark-etl
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 2Gi
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: 512Mi
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Never
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      serviceAccount: spark
      serviceAccountName: spark
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30

  active: 1

I can see the current ETL job already has the fix applied (`--conf spark.executor.memory=1g`, `--conf spark.sql.adaptive.enabled=true`, `--conf spark.sql.adaptive.skewJoin.enabled=true`). 

Wait - this is interesting. The job already appears to have the fix applied. Let me re-read the task description more carefully.

Looking at the `last-applied-configuration`, I can see the job was already created with `spark.executor.memory=1g` and AQE enabled. But the task says the problem is `--executor-memory 256m`. 

The comment in the YAML explains the issue clearly - the original 256m executor memory was causing OutOfMemoryError on the skewed Stage 3 aggregation, and the fix involves increasing it to 1g and enabling AQE skew handling. So the job I'm looking at already has these corrections applied. Let me check if this is the expected final state or if I need to trace back to see what the original problematic configuration was.
